title: Visiting Researcher Programme (VRP) 2026 in Italy – NATO CMRE Fully Funded Research Opportunity
summary: Are you a university researcher or graduate seeking hands-on experience in cutting-edge maritime science, defence innovation, artificial intelligence, robotics, cybersecurity, or ocean engineering? The Visiting Researcher Programme (VRP) 2026 at the NATO Centre for Maritime Research and Experimentation (CMRE) in La Spezia, Italy, offers a prestigious opportunity to work within a world-class scientific environment under the umbrella of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO). Applications are currently being accepted and will be reviewed on a monthly basis for existing project requirements. The final application deadline is September 30, 2026, at 10:59 PM. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the NATO CMRE Visiting Researcher Programme The CMRE is a globally recognized scientific research and experimentation facility dedicated to advancing maritime security and defence innovation for NATO member states. Through the Visiting Researcher Programme, CMRE supports its Research Division (RD) and Engineering and Information Technology Division (EITD) by welcoming talented students and researchers from NATO nations to contribute to ongoing scientific and technological initiatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Selected researchers join dynamic interdisciplinary teams, gain access to advanced equipment and experimental facilities, and collaborate with leading scientists and engineers. The programme is structured to align individual research interests with NATO s strategic priorities in maritime security and technological advancement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Assignments last a minimum of 90 consecutive days and may be extended once for up to an additional 90 days. In some cases, shorter assignments under three months may be considered. Research Areas and Core Competencies The Visiting Researcher Programme covers a broad range of high-impact scientific fields, including: Underwater acoustics and seismic acoustics Underwater robotics and unmanned systems Ocean observation and prediction Sensors and signal processing Maritime security and operations research Artificial Intelligence and autonomy Cognitive science and collective intelligence Cybersecurity and cloud computing Modelling and simulation Quantum technology Climate change and security Data exploitation and information theory Computer science and networking Ocean and marine engineering Virtual reality and compact fuel cell power modules ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Current research initiatives include autonomous mine countermeasures, cooperative anti-submarine warfare, environmental knowledge systems, and operational effectiveness for maritime security. Applicants are strongly encouraged to review CMRE s ongoing technical projects and tailor their research proposals accordingly. Candidates may propose their own research topics, provided they align with CMRE s project needs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Salary, Benefits and Conditions The programme offers a monthly salary of 3,079.45 (NATO Grade G2) for Research Fellows. Undergraduate applicants may apply as Research Interns with a monthly salary of 1,375.46. Additional benefits include: Paid travel to and from La Spezia (for assignments longer than 30 days) Access to advanced research infrastructure Participation in NATO s Science and Technology Organization (STO) STEM community Collaboration with international experts ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Research Fellows are classified as Consultants under NATO regulations. Salaries are subject to taxation according to national legislation. Insurance coverage for health and accidents is mandatory and must be arranged by the researcher. Visa arrangements are the responsibility of the candidate. Researchers may be required to obtain a NATO Secret security clearance prior to arrival, depending on the assignment. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Eligibility Criteria To apply, candidates must: Hold a Bachelor s or Master s degree in a related field Demonstrate English proficiency (SLP 3333) Be at least 21 years old Be a national of a NATO member state Applications are strictly limited to nationals of NATO member countries, including the United States, United Kingdom, Canada, Germany, France, Italy, Spain, T rkiye, Sweden, Finland, and other member states. Application Process Applications must be submitted through the NATO Talent Acquisition Program (NTAP) e-recruitment system. Applicants must include a CV, relevant diplomas, and key publications. This is an open candidate pool call, meaning applicants are not applying for a specific project but may be considered for assignments aligned with their profile. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); The selection process includes CV screening and a remote interview with subject matter experts. Final appointment decisions are made by the CMRE Director. Important: NATO does not permit the use of generative AI tools in any stage of the recruitment process. Applications prepared using AI tools may be rejected. The application deadline is September 30, 2026, at 10:59 PM. For more information click here Apply here Discover more opportunities here The post Visiting Researcher Programme (VRP) 2026 in Italy – NATO CMRE Fully Funded Research Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded PhD Positions 2026 in Europe & Africa
summary: Are you planning to start your PhD in 2026? A new international doctoral call is now open, offering 15 fully funded PhD positions across leading universities in Europe and Africa. These three-year doctoral positions bring together interdisciplinary research on climate change, global health, epidemics, environmental toxicity, migration, biodiversity, mental health, and biosecurity. The PhD starting date for all selected candidates is September 1, 2026. All 15 positions are fully funded for three years, providing an exceptional opportunity for motivated researchers seeking to engage with urgent global challenges. Candidates are welcome to apply for more than one project. However, applications must be submitted directly to the university hosting each project, and applicants must carefully follow the specific application procedures outlined by the recruiting institution. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Participating Universities and Deadlines Six major universities are recruiting doctoral candidates under this call: Aarhus University (Denmark) is recruiting four Doctoral Candidates for Projects 2, 6, 8, and 10. The application deadline is March 15, 2026. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The University of Oslo (Norway) is recruiting three Doctoral Candidates for Projects 1, 9, and 14. The deadline is March 16, 2026. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); KU Leuven (Belgium) is recruiting two Doctoral Candidates for Projects 7 and 13. The deadline is March 11, 2026. The University of Edinburgh (United Kingdom) is recruiting two Doctoral Candidates for Projects 5 and 15. The deadline is March 16, 2026. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The University of Nairobi (Kenya) is recruiting two Doctoral Candidates for Projects 4 and 12. The deadline is March 15, 2026. The University of Cape Town (South Africa) is recruiting two Doctoral Candidates for Projects 3 and 11. Details about these projects will be released at a later date, and adequate time will be provided for proposal preparation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Because deadlines vary between March 11 and March 16, 2026, applicants are strongly encouraged to begin preparing their documents early. Research Themes and Projects The 15 PhD projects address interconnected global challenges at the intersection of environment, society, and health. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Key research topics include agricultural intensification and health transformations; climate change, migration, and elderly care; environmental toxicity and well-being; biodiversity and zoonotic diseases; epidemics and state formation in Africa; youth migration and climate adaptation; urban toxicity in extractive landscapes; gendered cancer epidemics; infrastructures of toxic evidence; biosecurity and food-related health risks; non-communicable diseases and environmental repair; urban commons and health infrastructures; youth mental health and gambling epidemics in times of environmental crisis; epidemics and natural disasters as economic enterprises; and vector-borne disease expertise and response in Africa and Europe. These projects are highly interdisciplinary, combining public health, anthropology, environmental studies, epidemiology, social sciences, governance research, and development studies. Successful candidates will work within international research teams and benefit from cross-continental collaboration between European and African institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Funding and Benefits All positions are fully funded for three years. Funding typically covers tuition fees and provides a competitive doctoral salary or stipend in accordance with the regulations of the recruiting university. Candidates will gain access to high-quality research training, international academic networks, mobility opportunities, and strong supervision within globally recognized institutions. Beyond financial support, these PhD positions offer the opportunity to conduct impactful research addressing some of the most pressing issues of our time, including climate crisis, epidemic preparedness, environmental justice, and global health inequalities. How to Apply Applicants must apply directly to the university hosting their selected project. Each university has its own application portal, required documents, and eligibility criteria. Generally, candidates are expected to hold a relevant Master s degree or equivalent in a related field and demonstrate strong academic performance and research motivation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Because you may apply for more than one project, it is important to tailor each application carefully to the specific research focus and supervisory team. Ensure that all required documents, such as your CV, motivation letter, academic transcripts, references, and any project-specific materials, are prepared well in advance. The PhD starting date is September 1, 2026. With deadlines between March 11 and March 16, 2026, this is a competitive and time-sensitive opportunity. Early preparation and careful review of each university s application instructions will significantly strengthen your chances of success. for more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Fully Funded PhD Positions 2026 in Europe Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Netherlands-Sponsored UN JPO Vacancies 2026: 30+ Junior Professional Officer Positions Across WHO, FAO, UNICEF, OHCHR, OCHA, ILO, IFAD, IOM & More
summary: The Nedworc Foundation has announced a new round of Netherlands-sponsored Junior Professional Officer (JPO) vacancies for 2026, with approximately 30 new positions becoming available between January and March 2026. These highly competitive United Nations JPO positions are funded by the Government of the Netherlands and offer early-career professionals the opportunity to work with leading UN agencies across the globe, including WHO, FAO, IFAD, UNICEF, OCHA, OHCHR, ILO, IOM, EOSG, and the Resident Coordinator System (RCS). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Vacancy announcements are published on the Nedworc Foundation website, LinkedIn, and relevant UN agency websites. Each position has a specific deadline, with most applications closing between 2 March and 31 March 2026. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); For professionals seeking to launch or advance a career within the United Nations system, these JPO roles provide a structured pathway into international development, humanitarian affairs, human rights, climate action, political affairs, and sustainable development coordination. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); What Is the Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme? The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme is a career development initiative that allows governments to sponsor young professionals to work within the United Nations system. Under the Netherlands-sponsored JPO scheme: The Government of the Netherlands funds selected candidates. Professionals are placed in UN agencies worldwide. JPOs gain hands-on experience in international policymaking, programme implementation, and multilateral coordination. Assignments are typically for a fixed-term period. Participants build strong networks within the UN system. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The Nedworc Foundation manages the recruitment process for these Dutch-sponsored JPO positions. 2026 Netherlands-Sponsored JPO Vacancies (Open Positions) Below are current openings and their respective deadlines: WHO Health Emergency Officer (Medical Evacuations) Location: Jerusalem, Occupied Palestinian Territory Open to: Dutch nationals Closes: 29 March 2026 FAO JPO Climate Change and Food Security Location: Jakarta, Indonesia Open to: Nationals from eligible countries Closes: 27 March 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); IFAD Private Sector Portfolio Analyst Location: Rome, Italy Open to: Dutch nationals Closes: 31 March 2026 RCS JPO in Sustainable Development Coordination (Private Sector Engagement) Location: Accra, Ghana Open to: Dutch nationals Closes: 10 March 2026 EOSG JPO in Political Affairs Location: New York, USA Open to: Dutch nationals Closes: 10 March 2026 EOSG JPO in Communications (Political Affairs) Location: New York, USA Open to: Nationals from eligible countries Closes: 10 March 2026 OCHA JPO in Field Coordination Location: Mogadishu, Somalia Open to: Nationals from eligible countries Closes: 10 March 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); OHCHR JPO in Human Rights Location: Geneva, Switzerland Open to: Nationals from eligible countries Closes: 10 March 2026 OHCHR JPO in Human Rights (LGBTI People, Gender Equality, Women s Rights) Location: Geneva, Switzerland Open to: Nationals from eligible countries Closes: 10 March 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); OCHA JPO in Humanitarian Affairs Location: Kabul, Afghanistan Open to: Dutch nationals Closes: 10 March 2026 OHCHR JPO in Human Rights Location: Damascus, Syria Open to: Dutch nationals Closes: 10 March 2026 ILO JPO in Fundamental Principles and Rights at Work Location: Bangkok, Thailand Open to: Nationals from eligible countries Closes: 5 March 2026 IOM Programme Officer (SAR/SARD) Location: Tripoli, Libya Open to: Dutch nationals Closes: 4 March 2026 UNICEF WASH Officer Location: Gaza, State of Palestine Open to: Dutch nationals Closes: 2 March 2026 UNICEF Emergency Officer Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia Open to: Nationals from eligible countries Closes: 2 March 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Areas of Focus These JPO roles span multiple thematic areas, including: Health emergency coordination Climate change and food security Human rights and gender equality Political affairs and communications Sustainable development coordination Humanitarian field coordination Labour rights and fundamental principles Migration management Water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH) Emergency response programming This breadth allows candidates from diverse academic and professional backgrounds to find alignment with their expertise. Why Apply for a Netherlands-Sponsored JPO Position? 1. Direct Entry into the UN System The JPO Programme is one of the most structured entry pathways into long-term UN careers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); 2. International Exposure Assignments are located across Africa, the Middle East, Europe, Asia, and North America. 3. Government Sponsorship Funding is provided by the Government of the Netherlands, reducing financial barriers to entry. 4. Professional Development JPOs gain: Practical experience in multilateral diplomacy Policy drafting and programme implementation skills Access to senior-level mentorship Global networking opportunities Application Process Vacancy announcements are published on: The Nedworc Foundation website LinkedIn Relevant UN agency websites Applicants must: Carefully review eligibility (nationality requirements vary per vacancy). Submit required documents through the specified UN or Nedworc recruitment channels. Apply before the stated deadline (most close between 2 March and 31 March 2026). Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive nature of these roles. Visit OFY HERE for more global opportunities. title: Nominate a Transformational Leader for the 2026 Tällberg-SNF-Eliasson Global Leadership Prize
summary: Are you inspired by a leader who demonstrates courage, innovation, and unwavering ethical commitment in addressing global challenges? Nominations are now open for the 2026 T llberg-SNF-Eliasson Global Leadership Prize an internationally recognized award celebrating exceptional individuals who are shaping a better future for humanity. Leaders from any country, sector, or discipline are eligible. If you know someone who is redefining leadership through action and integrity, this is your opportunity to ensure their work receives global recognition. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Deadline: March 31, 2026, at midnight HST (GMT -10). About the Prize The T llberg-SNF-Eliasson Global Leadership Prize is awarded to outstanding leaders whose work reflects bold thinking, moral clarity, and meaningful global impact. The Prize recognizes individuals who understand that today s most pressing challenges climate change, inequality, geopolitical instability, technological disruption, and social fragmentation require cross-border collaboration and human-centered solutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Rather than rewarding position or title, the Prize honors those who act decisively and ethically, often in complex and uncertain environments. Laureates join a distinguished global network committed to advancing dialogue, cooperation, and sustainable impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); What Kind of Leaders Are Being Sought? The Prize looks for individuals who: Demonstrate innovative and courageous leadership. Operate with integrity and strong ethical foundations. Work in diverse fields but recognize that global problems demand global solutions. Take initiative instead of waiting for political systems to resolve challenges. Reject divisive ideologies and focus instead on thoughtful, people-centered action. Embody the uniquely human capacity to see beyond systems and structures to serve communities meaningfully. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Nominees may come from: Business and entrepreneurship Politics and governance Science and research Human rights and advocacy Technology and innovation Arts and media Civil society and community leadership Only individuals may be nominated organizations are not eligible. Eligibility Rules Anyone may nominate a leader, except Prize jurors and the candidates themselves. Self-nominations are not permitted. Nominees must be individuals (not institutions or organizations). The working language of the nomination process is English. Multiple nominations do not provide any advantage to a candidate. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Nominators are strongly encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible before the deadline. Nomination Process: 5 Simple Steps Provide your contact details and those of the nominee. Explain clearly how the nominee meets the leadership criteria. Highlight the nominee s major career achievements and milestones. Upload supporting documents such as CVs, articles, videos, or other relevant materials. Submit the completed nomination form before the deadline. The nomination form must be completed in English. Selection Timeline February 1 March 31, 2026 Nomination Period: Nominations are open worldwide. April October 2026 Selection Process: All nominations undergo rigorous research and evaluation. A pre-jury panel identifies outstanding candidates. In September, an international jury reviews finalists and conducts interviews. Following final deliberations in October, three Prize winners are selected. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); November 2026 Official Announcement: The three laureates of the 2026 Prize are publicly announced and recognized for their extraordinary leadership. January 2027 Celebration Global Recognition: Prize winners are honored at an international event and welcomed into the global T llberg leadership network, fostering long-term collaboration and influence. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Why This Prize Matters In a time of rising polarization and complex global crises, the world needs leaders who transcend narrow ideologies and champion universal values. The T llberg-SNF-Eliasson Global Leadership Prize serves as a platform to amplify such voices and inspire systemic change. By nominating a leader today, you are helping spotlight individuals who are building bridges, advancing solutions, and shaping a more ethical and sustainable global future. Submit your nomination before March 31, 2026, and contribute to recognizing leadership that truly makes a difference. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); For more information click here Apply here Discover more opportunities here The post Nominate a Transformational Leader for the 2026 Tällberg-SNF-Eliasson Global Leadership Prize appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fellowship 2026: BPRC Climate Policy Fellowship (Paid Remote Program) Apply Now
summary: Fellowship opportunities in climate policy are now open through the Building Power Resource Center (BPRC) for the BPRC Fellowship 2026, a paid six-month remote program designed for recent master s graduates interested in climate policy, organizing, and economic justice. Applications are open and must be submitted by 27 March 2026. The BPRC Fellowship provides early-career policy professionals with the opportunity to support community organizations, unions, and local governments working to implement ambitious climate solutions. Fellows will work on real projects focused on decarbonization policies, community power building, and equitable clean energy transitions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Through this fellowship, participants will collaborate with experienced organizers, policy experts, and government leaders who are working to build strong climate policies that deliver economic and environmental benefits for communities across the United States. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The program is especially designed for individuals who are passionate about climate action, economic justice, and democratic participation in policymaking. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); About the Fellowship The Building Power Resource Center (BPRC) is a strategy and technical assistance organization that works with grassroots coalitions, labor unions, and local governments to implement climate policies that benefit working communities. The organization focuses on building durable climate policies that combine long-term environmental goals with practical implementation strategies. BPRC promotes an inside-outside organizing approach where grassroots coalitions mobilize public support while government leaders design and implement policy solutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Through the BPRC Fellowship, participants will work directly with BPRC partners on projects related to decarbonization, community energy programs, and public sector climate initiatives. The fellowship aims to build a pipeline of policy professionals who understand both grassroots organizing and government policy implementation. Fellows gain practical experience in research, policy analysis, campaign strategy, and coalition building. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Benefits The BPRC Fellowship offers several benefits for participants seeking to build careers in climate policy and public sector innovation. Key benefits include: $4,000 monthly stipend 20 hours per week work schedule Four-day workweek during summer months (June August) Opportunity to work with leading climate policy organizers and government officials Direct involvement in high-impact decarbonization initiatives Mentorship from experienced climate policy professionals Hands-on experience in policy implementation and coalition building ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Fellows also gain exposure to policy strategies that are being replicated across the United States to accelerate the transition toward a clean energy economy. Focus Areas The BPRC Fellowship focuses on policy initiatives that support climate action while strengthening democratic participation and community power. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Key policy areas include: Public school decarbonization initiatives Neighborhood-scale clean energy programs Community climate policy campaigns Equitable green energy transition initiatives Union job creation within the clean energy sector Public sector climate policy implementation These initiatives aim to reduce climate pollution while improving public health outcomes, lowering energy costs, and creating sustainable employment opportunities. Eligibility The BPRC Fellowship is designed for individuals with strong interests in climate policy, organizing, and social change. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Applicants should ideally meet the following criteria: Recent graduate with a master s degree in policy, urban planning, public administration, or related fields Experience in organizing, campaigning, or policy development Strong communication and collaboration skills Ability to work independently and manage projects effectively Demonstrated commitment to equity, justice, and community empowerment Understanding of political dynamics within climate policy work Experience with decarbonization policy or energy systems is helpful but not required. Applicants must also be located in the United States and have valid work authorization. Duration Location The fellowship will run for six months, beginning in June 2026 and ending in November 2026. Key details include: Duration: June 2026 November 2026 Work schedule: 20 hours per week Location: Remote (United States) Stipend: $4,000 per month ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Some fellows may travel once or twice during the fellowship depending on project requirements. How to Apply Applicants must submit their application through the official online application portal before the deadline. The application process requires: A resume Responses to two application questions After applications close, selected candidates will be invited to participate in interviews during April 2026. The fellowship selection timeline includes: Application deadline: 27 March 2026 Interview period: April 2026 Offers sent: Early May 2026 Fellowship start date: June 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); APPLY HERE Deadline 27 March 2026 Final Note The BPRC Fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity for early-career policy professionals to gain practical experience in climate policy, community organizing, and public sector governance. By working alongside grassroots leaders and government partners, fellows will contribute to projects that advance decarbonization while strengthening democratic institutions and economic equity. For individuals passionate about climate justice and policy innovation, this fellowship provides a unique pathway into impactful climate leadership roles. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); For more opportunities click here The post Fellowship 2026: BPRC Climate Policy Fellowship (Paid Remote Program) Apply Now appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Women & Girls Grants 2026: Community Fund for Women & Girls
summary: Women-focused nonprofit organizations in Greater New Haven can now apply for Women Girls Grants through the Community Fund for Women Girls, which offers funding of up to $15,000 to support programs that empower women and girls. Applications are currently open and must be submitted by 23 March 2026 at 5:00 PM. The Community Foundation for Greater New Haven launched this grant initiative to strengthen programs that improve the economic security, leadership opportunities, education, and health outcomes of women and girls in the region. Through this funding opportunity, nonprofit organizations can receive financial support to expand their services, strengthen advocacy efforts, and implement initiatives that address structural barriers affecting women and girls. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The Women Girls funding initiative prioritizes programs that demonstrate measurable community impact and focus on long-term empowerment and equity. Nonprofit organizations working in areas such as economic autonomy, leadership development, educational advancement, and health and wellness are encouraged to apply. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Women Girls Grant Opportunity The Community Fund for Women Girls was established to invest in programs that address systemic inequalities affecting women and girls across Greater New Haven communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Through this grant program, nonprofit organizations can receive funding of up to $15,000 to support direct services, advocacy initiatives, or community organizing efforts that improve opportunities and outcomes for women and girls. The goal of the fund is to empower organizations that are actively creating pathways toward economic independence, leadership opportunities, educational achievement, and improved health outcomes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); By investing in community-driven solutions, the program seeks to strengthen local initiatives that support gender equity and help women and girls thrive socially, economically, and educationally. Benefits Organizations selected for the Women Girls grant program will receive several key benefits, including: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Funding of up to $15,000 per project Support for direct services and community-based initiatives Opportunities to expand gender-focused advocacy programs Resources to strengthen community organizing and policy engagement Increased visibility and recognition through the Community Foundation network This funding helps nonprofits scale their initiatives and deepen their impact in communities where women and girls face barriers to opportunity and wellbeing. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Focus Areas The Community Fund for Women Girls supports projects that align with four key impact pathways designed to improve the lives of women and girls. Economic Autonomy Programs focused on strengthening the financial independence and economic mobility of women and girls are strongly encouraged. Eligible initiatives may include: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Access to safe and affordable housing Entrepreneurship programs for women Workforce participation and employment support Direct cash assistance initiatives Childcare innovations and family-friendly policy support Advocacy and community organizing for economic security These initiatives aim to create sustainable economic opportunities and reduce financial barriers faced by women and families. Leadership Leadership development programs help women and girls become influential decision-makers in their communities and institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Examples of supported initiatives include: Leadership training and mentorship programs Women s leadership development in business, government, and nonprofit sectors Advocacy and community organizing skill-building Programs promoting equity-centered policy leadership These initiatives aim to empower women and girls to shape policies and decisions that affect their lives. Education Educational programs supported by the Women Girls grant program focus on increasing access to learning opportunities and academic success. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Eligible initiatives may include: Support for women and girls entering underrepresented academic fields Programs assisting first-generation or nontraditional students Educational access initiatives for student parents Advocacy and policy initiatives supporting educational equity Education remains a critical pathway to long-term economic and social empowerment for women and girls. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Health Wellness Health and safety programs that improve the wellbeing of women and girls are also a key funding priority. Examples include: Access to reproductive health education and services Maternal health and postpartum support programs Initiatives preventing violence against women and girls Services supporting survivors of gender-based violence Advocacy programs promoting women s health rights These initiatives help ensure women and girls have the resources and support necessary to live healthy and safe lives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Eligibility To qualify for the Women Girls grant funding, applicants must meet the following criteria: Be a nonprofit organization serving Greater New Haven communities Have at least 18 24 months of continuous programming experience Align with at least one of the four impact areas outlined in the program Serve residents within the Community Foundation s 20-town region Be up to date with reporting requirements if previously funded by the Community Foundation The fund supports organizations serving women and girls, including individuals who identify as cisgender, transgender, or nonbinary. Duration Location The funding supports nonprofit programs operating within Greater New Haven, Connecticut (United States). Key timeline details include: Application deadline: 23 March 2026 Award announcements: May 2026 Funding amount: Up to $15,000 per project How to Apply Eligible nonprofit organizations can apply for funding by completing the official grant application before the deadline. Application steps: Review the Community Foundation s general eligibility guidelines. Prepare a proposal aligned with one of the four impact pathways. Submit the application through the official grant portal. Await the evaluation process and funding decision. Applications must clearly demonstrate how the proposed program will benefit women and girls within the Greater New Haven community. title: Conservation Sisters Fellowship 2026 in Zimbabwe: One-Year Conservation Internship for Recent Female Graduates
summary: The Wildlife Conservation Action Conservation Sisters Fellowship 2026 is now open for applications, offering a unique one-year internship opportunity for recent female graduates in Zimbabwe who are seeking to begin their careers in wildlife conservation and environmental management. This fellowship is specifically designed to empower young women by providing hands-on field experience, mentorship, and professional development within leading conservation organizations in Zimbabwe. If you are passionate about wildlife, environmental sustainability, and conservation work but have not yet gained formal employment in the sector, this program provides a structured entry point into the field. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); With an application deadline of 06 March 2026, eligible candidates are encouraged to apply promptly. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Overview of the Conservation Sisters Fellowship 2026 Program Name: Conservation Sisters Fellowship 2026 Host Organization: Wildlife Conservation Action Location: Zimbabwe Contract Type: One-Year Internship Sector: Conservation / Wildlife Management Application Deadline: 06 March 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The Conservation Sisters Fellowship is a targeted capacity-building initiative aimed at addressing the gender gap in conservation by equipping young female graduates with the skills, networks, and practical exposure required to thrive in the field. About Wildlife Conservation Action Wildlife Conservation Action is committed to strengthening conservation initiatives and building local capacity in Zimbabwe s wildlife and environmental management sector. Through structured internships and fellowship programs, the organization supports emerging professionals in gaining the expertise needed to contribute meaningfully to biodiversity protection and sustainable environmental stewardship. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The Conservation Sisters Fellowship represents a strategic investment in young women who demonstrate potential and commitment to conservation work. Purpose and Objectives of the Fellowship The Conservation Sisters Fellowship is designed to: Provide structured, hands-on experience in wildlife and conservation programs Equip young women with technical and operational skills in conservation management Facilitate mentorship and professional networking Build confidence and leadership potential within the conservation sector Strengthen Zimbabwe s conservation ecosystem through youth participation ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); This program is particularly beneficial for graduates who have recently completed their studies and are seeking to transition from academic learning to practical, field-based conservation work. Key Responsibilities of Fellows Selected Fellows will engage in a broad range of activities designed to provide comprehensive exposure to conservation operations. Responsibilities may include: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Practical Field Experience Fellows will gain direct involvement in wildlife and conservation programs, allowing them to understand ecological management, species protection, habitat preservation, and environmental monitoring. Participation in Training and Mentorship The program includes structured mentorship from experienced conservation professionals. Fellows will participate in skill-building workshops, field demonstrations, and operational briefings. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Operational Support Fellows will assist in the daily operations of leading conservation organizations, gaining insights into administrative processes, project management, compliance standards, and stakeholder engagement. Contribution to Ongoing Projects Participants will actively contribute to conservation initiatives and environmental projects across Zimbabwe, ensuring meaningful involvement rather than observational learning. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Conservation Sisters Fellowship 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a recent graduate within the last 12 months Be female Have no prior formal employment in the conservation sector (industrial attachments are excluded from this restriction) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Demonstrate strong motivation to learn and contribute to practical conservation work This opportunity is specifically tailored for early-career women seeking to break into the conservation industry without prior professional experience. Entry Point into Conservation Many conservation roles require prior field experience. Empowering Women in Conservation The program prioritizes female participation, contributing to gender equity in wildlife and environmental leadership. 3. Professional Development Participants build practical competencies that go beyond academic training, positioning themselves competitively in the job market. 4. title: Disability Jobs: EU Council Pilot Scheme for Persons with Disabilities 2026
summary: Disability Jobs opportunities are now open through the European Council s pilot scheme aimed at increasing employment opportunities for persons with recognised disabilities across the European Union. The Council of the European Union has launched a new recruitment initiative that allows candidates with recognised disabilities to register in a dedicated database and express interest in working as contract agents within the institution. This new initiative is part of the Council s Diversity and Inclusion Strategy 2030, which seeks to create a more inclusive workforce and ensure equal opportunities for individuals from all backgrounds. Through this programme, the Council aims to recruit talented professionals with disabilities who can contribute to EU policymaking, administration, communication, finance, and many other professional fields. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The pilot scheme will run for two years, with a general call for expressions of interest published annually. Each year, the Council intends to recruit up to three candidates with recognised disabilities for contract agent positions lasting one year. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Disability Jobs Opportunity The Disability Jobs pilot scheme introduces a dedicated recruitment database specifically designed for candidates with recognised disabilities. This database allows qualified individuals to express their interest in working with the Council of the European Union in various professional roles. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Unlike traditional EU recruitment systems such as the CAST Permanent (CAST P) database managed by the European Personnel Selection Office (EPSO), this initiative provides an additional and targeted recruitment pathway for people with disabilities. The initiative aims to improve representation, strengthen workplace inclusion, and ensure that talented professionals with disabilities have fair access to employment opportunities within EU institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Candidates selected through the scheme will work as contract agents, contributing to the Council s work across a variety of departments and policy areas. Benefits of the Disability Jobs Scheme Selected candidates will gain several professional and career benefits, including: Opportunity to work within EU institutions at the heart of European decision-making Professional experience in European public administration Exposure to policy development and EU governance Opportunity to contribute to meaningful projects affecting EU citizens Development of international career skills Experience working in a multicultural professional environment ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); This opportunity is particularly valuable for professionals interested in European policy, governance, administration, law, communication, or international affairs. Professional Areas and Work Profiles The Council offers positions in a wide range of professional profiles. Candidates may register under three main function groups depending on their qualifications and experience. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Function Group IV Administrators Administrators work on high-level policy and strategic responsibilities. Their duties may include: Advising on EU policies and strategies Supporting the EU decision-making process Managing policy and administrative projects Conducting policy analysis and research Translating, editing, and revising official documents Coordinating institutional activities Function Group III Assistants Assistants support administrative and operational tasks within the Council. Typical duties include: Financial and administrative support Coordinating work processes and programmes Drafting documents and reports Supporting internal procedures Managing documentation and workflow processes ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Function Group II Secretaries / Clerks Secretaries and clerks provide logistical and operational support across different departments. Responsibilities may include: Administrative and secretarial tasks Document management Organising meetings and communication Supporting finance, policy, and translation teams Providing logistical and technical assistance Eligibility Applicants interested in the Disability Jobs scheme must: Have a recognised disability Meet the eligibility requirements outlined in the official call Demonstrate the professional skills required for the selected function group Register through the EU Survey platform Candidates from various professional backgrounds are encouraged to apply, including individuals with experience in: Public administration Law and policy Communication Information technology Finance Translation Project management Duration Location The selected candidates will work as contract agents within the Council of the European Union, located primarily in Brussels, Belgium. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Key details include: Contract duration: 1 year Pilot programme length: 2 years Recruitment: Up to three candidates per year Work environment: International EU institution How to Apply To apply for the Disability Jobs pilot scheme: Visit the EU Survey application platform Register in the dedicated database for candidates with disabilities Complete the application form Submit your expression of interest APPLY HERE Once registered, your profile will be available for potential recruitment opportunities within the Council. Deadline Applications are currently open. The general call for expressions of interest was published on 3 November 2025, and eligible candidates can apply through the EU Survey platform. Final Note This initiative represents an important step toward building a more inclusive European workforce. By creating dedicated recruitment pathways, the Council aims to ensure that individuals with disabilities have equitable access to meaningful employment opportunities within EU institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); If you are interested in contributing to European policymaking while building an international career, the Disability Jobs scheme offers a valuable opportunity to become part of the European Union s professional community. For more opportunities click here https://opportunitiesforyouth.org The post Disability Jobs: EU Council Pilot Scheme for Persons with Disabilities 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
